back: The process of sending data from one device to another.
A small piece of data sent across a network.

front: Why is data split into packets?
back: To allow data to be transmitted efficiently and reliably.

front: What are the three parts of a packet?
back: Header, payload, trailer.

front: What is in the packet header?
back: Destination address, source address, packet number.
The actual data being sent.
Marks the end of the packet and includes error checking.
Packets are sent independently across a network and reassembled at the destination.

front: Why might packets arrive out of order?
back: They may take different routes through the network.
A router.
Data sent one bit at a time over a single wire.
Multiple bits sent at the same time over multiple wires.

front: What is an advantage of parallel transmission?
back: Faster over short distances.

front: What are disadvantages of parallel transmission?
back: Crosstalk, skew, expensive, not suitable for long distances.

front: What are advantages of serial transmission?
back: No crosstalk, no skew, cheaper, reliable over long distances.
Data flows in one direction only.
Data flows both ways but not at the same time.
Data flows both ways at the same time.
A standard interface that uses serial transmission to connect devices.
Provides power, plug and play, supports many devices.

front: What is a disadvantage of USB?
back: Limited cable length.
